steps.title: Use the pyramid formula
steps.content:
For a square pyramid with side length $s$, the base area is $s^2$. The volume formula is

$$V=\frac{1}{3}s^2h$$

Substitute $V=300$ and $h=9$.

$$300=\frac{1}{3}s^2(9)$$

steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content: Simplify the equation and use the positive square root because a side length is positive. $$300=3s^2$$ $$100=s^2$$ $$10=s$$ The answer is A.
